charity.s: $290M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.58% of revenue) via IKEA Foundation.
charity.focus: Poverty reduction, climate change, refugee support, child welfare, homelessness prevention

environment.s: ASC-certified farmed salmon across global menu. [ASC certified]

animals.s: CIWF Good Chicken Award (Europe) — Better Chicken Commitment compliant. ASC-certified farmed salmon across global menu. [CIWF Award · ASC certified]

guns.s: No federal firearms license on record for this company in the ATF FFL registry — no licensed firearms dealing or manufacturing.

privacy.s: Terms-of-service privacy grade E (very poor) via ToS;DR (tosdr.org), CC BY-SA 3.0 — transformed to TruNorth's scale.

wiki.extract: IKEA is a multinational conglomerate founded in Sweden that designs and sells ready-to-assemble furniture, household goods, and other related services.

health.s: 60 CPSC recalls (2 in past 24 months); latest hazard: the plastic fittings that attach the mirror to the wall can break.

governance.s: Wikidata: owned by Stichting INGKA Foundation. (Wikidata Q54078)

topHazards.label: The plastic fittings that attach the mirror to the wall can break, causing the mirror to fall, posing a laceration hazard to consumers.
The crib mattresses could create a gap between the mattress and crib ends larger than allowed by federal regulations, posing an entrapment hazard to infants.
The metal rod connecting the guard rail to the bed frame can break in use, posing a laceration hazard.
Small metal pieces can detach from the garlic press, posing a laceration or ingestion hazard to consumers.

sampleRecalls.title: IKEA Recalls Garlic Presses Due to Laceration and Ingestion Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves IKEA 365+ VÄRDEFULL garlic presses used to crush garlic cloves. The garlic press has a black rubber handle and zinc coated garlic chamber. Only models with the IKEA logo are subject to the recall. The logo is located at the upper part of the handle.
hazards: Small metal pieces can detach from the garlic press, posing a laceration or ingestion hazard to consumers.
injuries: IKEA has received a total of 10 incident reports globally, including three reports of lacerations and finger splinters. No incidents or injuries have been reported in the U.S.
units: About 43,830 (in addition, about 10,700 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: IKEA Recalls VARMFRONT Power Banks Due to Fire Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves two IKEA VARMFRONT Power Banks with model numbers E2037 and E2038. The power banks are blue in color. Model E2037 has one USB-A port, one USB-C port, measures about four inches long and two inches wide, and has one of the following date codes (YYWW): 2318, 2319, 2322. Model E2038 has two USB-A por…
hazards: The power banks can overheat, posing a fire hazard.
injuries: IKEA has received three incident reports globally. No injuries have been reported. Additionally, no incidents or injuries have been reported in the U.S.
units: About 1,600 (In addition, about 1,690 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: IKEA Recalls USB Chargers Due to Burn and Electric Shock Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves IKEA ÅSKSTORM 40W USB Chargers. The product consists of a charger and a detachable power cord with a C7 connector and a plug. Model number ICPSW5-40-1 is printed on the label on the back side of the gray USB charger.
hazards: The USB charger's cable can become damaged or break over time, posing burn and electric shock hazards.
injuries: IKEA has received 17 incident reports globally, including five reports of burns and four reports of electric shock. No incidents or injuries have been reported in the U.S.
units: About 22,600 (In addition, about 10,250 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: IKEA Expands Recall of Mirrors Due to Laceration Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves all sizes of LETTAN flat mirrors. The mirrors are frameless, about 38 inches high and were sold in 23, 31, 39 and 47 inches wide. LETTAN mirrors affected by this recall for repair now include: all LETTAN mirrors with a date stamp before and including 2105 (YYWW), and LETTAN mirrors with supplier n…
hazards: The plastic fittings that attach the mirror to the wall can break, causing the mirror to fall, posing a laceration hazard to consumers.
injuries: IKEA has received 10 incident reports globally in addition to the 55 reported in the previous recall, and one report of the plastic fittings coming loose and the mirror falling off the wall in the U.…
units: About 14,600 (In addition, about 10,500 were sold in Canada) (About 22,400 were previously recalled in March 2023)

sampleRecalls.title: IKEA Recalls BLÅVINGAD Fishing Games Due to Choking Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves the IKEA BLÅVINGAD fishing game. The article name ("BLÅVINGAD"), article number "10539622," supplier number "21576," and "Made in China" are located on the back of the blue box. The ocean-themed fishing game consists of two wooden fishing lines with string and magnets to attract wooden play pieces…
hazards: A small metal rivet within a wooden play piece can come loose and detach, posing a choking hazard to young children.
injuries: Globally, IKEA has received notice of four incident reports of the rivet coming loose. None of the reported incidents occurred in the U.S. or required medical attention.
units: About 25,000 (In addition, about 2,200 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was driving a forklift when the forklift and his left hand struck a racking system, resulting in a cut and fracture that required surgery.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slipping
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was working at a cash register. The floor was wet and the mat the employee was standing on slipped out from under her feet. The employee fell on the floor and sustained wrist and back injuries.

sampleRecords.event_description: Nonroadway collision with object other than vehicle, unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: On January 18, 2023, an employee was operating a pallet jack to move boxes when his hand slipped off the pallet jack and struck a box. The employee sustained a hand injury.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level, unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was working from an 8-foot A-frame ladder, repositioning a Wi-Fi box attached to a drop ceiling. As he pulled a ceiling tile toward himself, he lost his balance and fell back off the ladder. He suffered fractures to the ribs and back.

sampleRecords.part_of_body: Back, including spine, spinal cord, unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was pulling a mattress from a shelf. The strap on the mattress broke, causing the employee to strike the floor and sustain a back injury.
